ciso.title: CISO
painPoints: Board pressure to quantify cyber risk in dollar terms
Tool sprawl — too many vendors, inconsistent data
Talent shortage — can't hire enough skilled analysts
Regulatory compliance burden increasing (SEC 4-day disclosure)
Supply chain risk visibility gaps
orionValue: Risk quantification engine with real-time threat data → board-ready metrics
Consolidates 4-5 tools into one platform → vendor reduction
AI augments each analyst to 10x productivity → solves talent gap
Automated materiality assessment for SEC compliance → 4 hours not 4 days
Supply chain threat monitoring with vendor risk scoring
demoFlow: Start with executive dashboard — ROI metrics and risk posture
Show board-ready reporting — AI-generated executive summaries
Demonstrate tool consolidation — "Replace 5 logins with 1"
Walk through compliance automation — SEC disclosure workflow
End with customer proof points — similar org, similar results
objections: Too expensive → Show tool consolidation savings (net neutral)
Already have threat intel → Show AI investigation vs. manual comparison
Need internal buy-in → Provide champion arming materials
Long procurement cycle → Offer 30-day POV to prove value first
closingQuestions: What does your board expect to see in terms of cyber risk reporting?
How many tools is your team currently context-switching between?
What would it mean if your team could handle 3x the investigation volume?

soc-mgr.title: SOC Manager
painPoints: Alert fatigue — thousands of alerts, most false positives
Analyst burnout and high turnover (40%+ annually)
Manual investigation workflows taking hours per incident
Lack of dark web visibility and threat actor attribution
Integration challenges with existing SIEM/SOAR stack
orionValue: AI auto-triages 85% of alerts → analysts focus on real threats
Each analyst becomes 10x more effective → reduces burnout
30-second investigations vs. 45-minute manual process
Real-time dark web monitoring and automated attribution
Native SIEM/SOAR integrations — deploys alongside existing stack
demoFlow: Start with alert triage demo — show AI vs. manual side-by-side
Live investigation — paste a real IOC and watch Orion work
Show dark web monitoring — threat actor profiles in seconds
Demonstrate SIEM integration — how Orion fits their workflow
End with Cyber Operations Simulator — let them play it
objections: We have too many tools already → Orion replaces tools, not adds another
Our SIEM handles this → SIEM alerts, Orion investigates — they're complementary
Need to see it work with our data → Offer POV with their real IOCs
Team doesn't want to learn another tool → Show 30-second investigation — no learning curve
closingQuestions: How long does a typical investigation take your team today?
What's your analyst turnover rate? What would reducing it mean for your team?
If your team could handle 5x the alert volume without adding headcount, what would that unlock?

compliance.title: Compliance Officer
painPoints: Regulatory requirements changing rapidly across jurisdictions
Travel Rule compliance complexity
Sanctions screening accuracy and false positive rates
Audit trail and evidence preservation requirements
Cross-border reporting obligations
orionValue: Real-time regulatory monitoring across 50+ jurisdictions
Automated Travel Rule compliance workflows
99.8% sanctions screening accuracy with minimal false positives
Immutable audit trail with court-admissible evidence packaging
Cross-border reporting automation with jurisdiction-specific templates
demoFlow: Start with regulatory landscape — show jurisdiction coverage
Demo sanctions screening — accuracy and speed vs. current process
Walk through audit trail — evidence preservation for regulators
Show Travel Rule automation — compliance without friction
End with regulatory update alerts — "never miss a change"
objections: We already meet compliance requirements → Show where gaps exist (dark web, cross-chain)
Too complex to implement → 2-week deployment, dedicated compliance onboarding
Our regulator hasn't required this yet → Show jurisdictions already requiring it
Need approval from legal → Provide regulatory compliance documentation package
closingQuestions: How confident are you that your current screening catches cross-chain movements?
What happens when your regulator asks for investigation evidence from 6 months ago?
How many hours per week does your team spend on manual compliance reporting?

cto.title: CTO / VP Engineering
painPoints: API integration complexity with existing security stack
Data sovereignty and deployment flexibility requirements
Performance at scale — millions of entities, sub-second queries
Development team bandwidth for security tooling
Total cost of ownership across the security stack
orionValue: RESTful API with comprehensive documentation — integrate in days
On-prem, cloud, and hybrid deployment options — full data sovereignty
Graph database architecture handles billions of entities at sub-second speed
Zero development overhead — fully managed SaaS with self-service admin
Consolidation reduces total stack TCO by 30-50%
demoFlow: Start with API documentation — show developer experience
Demo integration speed — live API call from their environment
Show architecture diagram — scalability and performance
Walk through deployment options — match their requirements
End with TCO analysis — consolidation savings
objections: Our stack is locked down → Show API-first architecture, works alongside existing tools
Concerned about performance → Share benchmark data — sub-100ms query times
Build vs. buy → Show what it would take to build internally (years + millions)
Data residency concerns → Offer deployment in their preferred region/on-prem
closingQuestions: What does your current integration timeline look like for new security tools?
Where are you on the build vs. buy spectrum for investigation capabilities?
What would sub-second entity enrichment mean for your investigation workflows?

procurement.title: Procurement
painPoints: Budget constraints and approval processes
Vendor consolidation mandates
Contract terms and SLA requirements
Competitive bidding requirements
Total cost of ownership justification
orionValue: Net-neutral cost through tool consolidation — eliminates 3-5 existing subscriptions
Supports vendor consolidation strategy — one platform replaces many
Enterprise SLAs with 99.9% uptime guarantee and dedicated support
Transparent pricing — no hidden costs, no per-query fees
Clear ROI documentation with customer proof points for budget approval
demoFlow: Start with TCO comparison — current stack vs. Orion
Show tool consolidation matrix — what Orion replaces
Review contract terms — flexible, transparent, enterprise-grade
Present customer references — similar org size and industry
End with ROI calculator — show payback period
objections: Over budget → Show net savings from tool consolidation
Need 3 competitive bids → Provide comparison matrix vs. alternatives
Contract terms are rigid → Flexible terms available, discuss specifics
Need more references → Connect with 3 similar customers directly
Long approval cycle → Start with free POV to build the business case
closingQuestions: What tools could Orion potentially replace in your current security stack?
What does your typical vendor approval timeline look like?
Would it help to connect with a procurement peer at a similar organization who went through this process?
